LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING RE: DEFENDANTS GE CAPITAL MONEY BANK (FKA GE MONEY BANK) AND GECRB'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. THE DEMURRER TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF HAS LEAVE TO PLEAD THE CAUSE OF ACTION AGAINST DEFENDANT DEFENDANTS AND HIGHLIGHT WHAT SECTION OF THE CONSUMER REPORTING AGENCIES ACT SHE SEEKS TO RECOVER UNDER. IN ADDITION, IF PLAINTIFF SEEKS RELIEF UNDER THE INVESTIGATIVE CONSUMER REPORTING AGENCIES ACT-A SEPARATE STATUTORY SCHEME-PLAINTIFF SHOULD ALLEGE THAT CLAIM AS A DISTINCT CAUSE OF ACTION. THE DEMURRER TO THE DECLARATORY RELIEF CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO ALLEGE AN ACTUAL CONTROVERSY. THE COURT SUSTAINS WITH 20 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND THE DEMURRER TO THE ACCOUNTING CAUSE OF ACTION. TO STATE A CAUSE OF ACTION, ONLY THE SIMPLEST PLEADING IS REQUIRED: (1) THE FIDUCIARY RELATIONSHIP OR OTHER CIRCUMSTANCES APPROPRIATE TO THE REMEDY [CITATION], [AND] (2) A BALANCE DUE FROM THE DEFENDANT TO THE PLAINTIFF THAT CAN ONLY BE ASCERTAINED BY AN ACCOUNTING." (5 WITKIN, CAL. PROC. (5TH ED. 2008) PLEADING, 820, P. 236.) PLAINTIFF DOES NOT ALLEGE THAT A BALANCE IS DUE FROM DEFENDANT TO PLAINTIFF. (COMPLAINT, 17.) ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: A. Off Calendar Dec-16-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling RE: DEFENDANTS GE CAPITAL MONEY BANK (FKA GE MONEY BANK) and GECRB'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the 1st cause of action is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. Plaintiff has leave to plead the cause of action against defendant defendants and highlight what section of the Consumer Reporting Agencies Act she seeks to recover under. In addition, if plaintiff seeks relief under the Investigative Consumer Reporting Agencies Act-a separate statutory scheme-plaintiff should allege that claim as a distinct cause of action. The demurrer to the declaratory relief cause of action is sustained with leave to amend to allege an actual controversy. The Court sustains with 20 days leave to amend the demurrer to the accounting cause of action. To state a cause of action, only the simplest pleading is required: (1) The fiduciary relationship or other circumstances appropriate to the remedy [citation], [and] (2) A balance due from the defendant to the plaintiff that can only be ascertained by an accounting." (5 Witkin, Cal. Proc. (5th ed. 2008) Pleading, 820, p. 236.) Plaintiff does not allege that a balance is due from Defendant to Plaintiff. (Complaint, 17.) Order signed in open court. Judge: A. James Robertson II; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Court Reporter: Laura Martinez, CSR #11332
